purpose: add_rule_from_csv over the committed rules.csv fixture: the exact-match header row is skipped (pyreason.py:806-809), a full row parses a rule whose explicit [0.5,1] bound rides its body clause (the head bnd defaults [1,1] in the fingerprint) with truthy-string booleans 'no'/'false', an all-empty-params row auto-names through add_rule (rule_1), and a quoted row carries commas inside rule_text with infer_edges 'yes' and set_static '1' coerced true (_parse_bool_param, pyreason.py:692-725) — the fingerprint pins the three parsed rules in row order and the reason step consumes them (the infer_edges edge rule writes inferred team edges into the edge trace). The set_static flag itself is not rendered by the fingerprint — named unobserved; its surface sits with type:Rule.
provenance: docs/surface.md fn:add_rule_from_csv happy-full-row/happy-minimal-row/happy-header-present/bound-bool-truthy-strings/bound-quoted-commas; pyreason.py:753-866 at the pin; fixture harness/fixtures/rules.csv
